steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.filiam: 'Filiam' is a noun meaning 'daughter' in the accusative
hints.Satanas: 'Satanas' is a proper noun — the subject of the relative clause
hints.Hanc: 'Hanc' is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' in the accusative feminine

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'alligat' — 'alligavit' has the perfect -avi- stem
hints.pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect would be 'alligaverat' — 'alligavit' is simple perfect

hints.lego, legere, legi, lectum: These are the parts of 'legere' (to read/gather)
hints.ligo, ligare, ligavi, ligatum: These are the parts of 'ligare' (to bind) — the simple verb; 'alligare' adds 'ad-'
hints.eligo, eligere, elegi, electum: These are the parts of 'eligere' (to choose)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'filia' — the -am ending marks accusative

hints.dative: 'Abrahae' could be dative, but here it shows possession — 'daughter of Abraham'

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'anni' — 'annis' is dative/ablative plural
hints.dative: 'Annis' could be dative, but here it expresses duration of time — ablative
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'annorum' — 'annis' is ablative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'anni' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'anni' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'anni' ends in -i

steps.prompt: What is the function of 'quam' in this sentence?
steps.target: relative pronoun (acc. fem. sg.) referring back to 'filiam'
choices: relative pronoun (acc. fem. sg.) referring back to 'filiam'

hints.conjunction meaning 'than': 'Quam' can mean 'than' after comparatives, but here it introduces a relative clause
hints.exclamatory adverb meaning 'how': 'Quam' can be exclamatory, but here it refers to 'filiam' as object of 'alligavit'
hints.interrogative pronoun: An interrogative 'quam' would introduce a question — this is a statement

translation: This daughter of Abraham, whom Satan has bound for eighteen years
